summary: Phase 4 (ballroom floor-plan canvas) backend + frontend tested end-to-end. All 12 new backend tests pass. All key frontend flows verified: canvas open/close/reopen, palette add (stage object + auto-incrementing round table), zoom in/out/fit, double-click opens TableDetailModal, drag-and-drop table with persistence after reload, floor-plan upload + persistence + removal, ✕ delete object with confirm dialog.
action_items: Consider fixing the operator-precedence bug in /app/frontend/src/pages/BallroomCanvas.jsx line 81: `dx: x - item.canvasX ?? x - item.x` is parsed as `(x - item.canvasX) ?? (x - item.x)`. When item.canvasX is undefined (canvas objects have x/y, not canvasX/canvasY), the subtraction returns NaN (not nullish), so the fallback to `item.x` never triggers; dx/dy become NaN. Fix: `dx: x - (item.canvasX ?? item.x), dy: y - (item.canvasY ?? item.y)`. In practice my test still worked because the drag handler treats the object position by `item.x` in setDrag.current, and the snap(NaN) propagation may not show up on small mouse moves — but this should be cleaned up. Note: in `current: { x: item.canvasX ?? item.x, y: item.canvasY ?? item.y }` the parentheses ARE correct, so the new position state is fine; only dx/dy offset is wrong, causing the object to 'jump' on first drag.
Provide a non-admin staff seed account in /app/memory/test_credentials.md so palette-hidden / upload-hidden behavior can be UI-tested (only admin Eitanp exists today).
critical_code_review_comments: BallroomCanvas.jsx L81: nullish-coalescing precedence bug as described above — dx/dy can be NaN for canvas_objects.
BallroomCanvas.jsx uses native window.confirm() (lines 143, 164) and window.alert() (lines 130, 138, 150, 157) — fine for now but inconsistent with shadcn Dialog/Toast used elsewhere.

BallroomCanvas.jsx onSvgPointerDown precedence (L85): `e.target === svgRef.current || e.target.tagName === 'rect' && e.target.getAttribute('data-bg') === '1'` — JS evaluates && before ||, so this works as intended, but parentheses would make it readable.

server.py set_floor_plan: allows arbitrary length data URLs in TEXT column (no server-side cap). For 4MB images this is many DB rows of base64. Consider migrating to object storage or capping payload size at the FastAPI layer.

server.py update_canvas_object always writes position+dimensions JSONB even when no x/y/width/height fields are sent (the `sets` list always includes them). Functionally OK because it re-writes the merged dict, but it's a minor inefficiency.
test_credentials: Admin Eitanp / Gpepeitan!23 (only account). No non-admin test user exists, so the 'non-admin sees no palette/upload' assertion could not be verified end-to-end — code inspection confirms `isAdmin` is gated on the palette panel (L204) and upload-floor-plan button (L185).
seed_data_creation: test_phase4.py auto-uses ballroom id=1 (existing 'Grand Ballroom'). It creates a temporary canvas_object (deleted within the same test class) and a temporary table prefixed TEST_P4_Table (deleted at end of test_patch_table_canvas_coords). UI test left a 'STAGE' canvas_object and one auto-added round table behind on ballroom 1.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 4 endpoints (PATCH /ballrooms/{id}/floor-plan, GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /canvas-objects, PATCH /tables/{id} canvasX/Y) all green. UI: drag-and-drop tables persist canvas_x/canvas_y across reload (verified table id=10 went from ~(?, ?) to translate(1000, 560) after a mouse drag). Floor plan upload stores data URL in background_image_url and survives reload. The biggest residual concern is the L81 nullish-coalescing bug for canvas_object drags — manual testing of dragging stage/bar/buffet rectangles around the canvas would be a good targeted retest after fix.
rca of the issue: N/A — no production bugs blocked the test. Single code-review nit on dx/dy computation documented in action_items.
